title: Graduate Sales Executive (Benelux Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in Dutch and English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: HR Executive (Fixed Term Contract)
description: **HR Executive** ***12 month Fixed Term Contract*** ***Our vision is to make net zero a reality by solving the commercial mechanics*** ***of a decarbonised energy system.*** ElectroRoute is an international, renewables focused, energy trading and services company. The company employs just under 100 energy professionals based in Ireland, the UK and Europe and is a subsidiary of Mitsubishi Corporation. ElectroRoute intelligently leverages the benefits of new technologies, our advanced methodologies, and the application of unrivalled expertise to develop innovative energy trading and renewable risk management solutions. These mitigate the factors which impede clean energy investment and help make net zero a reality. Our expert trading team operates 24x7 in Ireland, the UK, across Continental Europe and in Japan; utilising our bespoke trading platform, ElectroRoute CORE, to provide essential trading services to wind farms, solar farms, and batteries. The company is a significant commodity trader on its own account – trading a range of energy commodities including Power and Gas, in the global energy markets with activities spanning Europe to Japan. Our ambitious growth plans will see us increasing the volume and variety of our activities over the coming years, requiring growth in the strength and depth of our team to help us make net zero a reality. ElectroRoute is seeking to appoint an HR Executive on a 12\-month fixed\-term contract to provide maternity cover within the HR team. The successful candidate will be responsible for payroll and benefits administration, while also delivering broad HR support across the organisation. **The Position** **Payroll Processing / Benefit Management** * You will be responsible for the accurate, timely, and compliant delivery of payroll across designated countries or entities, acting as a key payroll specialist who will own end\-to\-end payroll processing which iincludes the review and validation of payroll inputs and outputs, coordination of payroll calendars and statutory deadlines and the processing of all payroll changes such as new hires, terminations, salary changes, bonuses, benefits, leaves. * Prepare and validate payroll submissions for our payroll providers * Completing salary analysis and preparing journals for finance. * Ensure full compliance with payroll legislation, including tax, pensions, BIK, and other statutory obligations * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date payroll and employee records on payroll and HRIS. * Respond to payroll queries from employees, providing clear explanations and timely resolutions * Work closely with HR and Finance teams to ensure seamless payroll integration and data accuracy * Carry out payroll audits and investigate and resolve discrepancies * Administration, reconciliation and payment of third\-party payroll deductions to relevant pension and healthcare providers * Completion of forms relating to Department of Social Protection and mortgage applications **HR Executive** * Support and promote the company culture by championing organisational values across the business. * Respond promptly and professionally to employee HR queries. * Coordinate recruitment activities for internship, temporary, and permanent roles. * Support the onboarding process for new hires, including contract preparation and ensuring a smooth transition into the organisation. * Administer employee benefits, including healthcare, pension, and related schemes. * Prepare and issue employee documentation across all stages of the employment lifecycle. * Maintain accurate HR records, reporting, and documentation within BambooHR. * Provide general HR administrative support, including employee letters and document management. * Coordinate internal company communications and HR\-related sessions as required. * Manage recruitment administration, including job advertising, agency coordination, interview scheduling, and interview support. * Support employees with visa and work permit administration where required. * Assist in delivering employee wellbeing initiatives and engagement activities. * Deliver induction training, performance management training, and other HR training programmes. * Manage relationships with external HR vendors and service providers. * Work collaboratively with the HR team to foster employee engagement, team spirit, and a positive workplace culture, including supporting company events and celebrations. **The Candidate** * Minimum of 3 – 5 years HR or payroll experience * Third level qualification in Payroll, HR, Business or related discipline is desirable. * Good planning and organisational skills * High level of accuracy and exceptional attention to detail * Ability to work effectively under pressure and meet strict deadlines * Proficient in MS Office, particularly Excel * Strong administrative and document management skills * Use of Bamboo system or other HRIS * Flexible and eager to learn material in new fields and develop new work practices * Ability to work independently while contributing positively as part of a team **Personal Attributes** * **Proactive and Independent** : Ability to take initiative and work autonomously while managing various tasks. * **Professionalism** : A professional attitude and approach, both internally with colleagues and externally with clients and vendors. * **Adaptability** : Flexibility to adapt to changing environments and manage unforeseen challenges. * **Confidentiality** : Ability to handle sensitive information discreetly and professionally. ***The role will be based in Dublin and will be hybrid (3 days in the office)*** ElectroRoute is an equal opportunity employer. We aim to create value through diversity and inclusion. We seek to attract candidates to reflect the diverse nature of our society. Diversity and inclusion allow the ElectroRoute team to be curious, creative, and promote positive change. We understand the many demands on all our employees, and we adopt flexible working arrangements where possible. Working at ElectroRoute offers something special for everyone.

title: Health & Safety Assistant (Graduate Position)
description: **ABOUT AMERICAN FRUITS AND FLAVORS** American Fruits and Flavors Ireland, DAC, (AFFI) has made its mark in the industry by processing the finest fruit juices, fruit concentrates, juice blends and spray dried powder versions of hundreds of unique fruit and flavor combinations for the food and beverage industry over 50 years. **POSITION OVERVIEW** Support with the development /collation and administration of programs and policies that result in Plant\-wide compliance with Health \& Safety statutory requirements. The position supports the safety function to create and maintain a safe and healthy working environment and assists the EHS coordinator with the management \& delivery of the Health \& Safety agenda and programmes for the site. Must contribute to the efficient and profitable operation of the Athy plant and maintain a positive work atmosphere, behaving and communicating in ways that foster good relationships with employees, co\-workers and managers, while achieving performance standards. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES \& DUTIES** 1\. Under the supervision and guidance of the EHS Coordinator: 1\.1\. Maintain the company’s Health \& Safety Management System 1\.2\. Assessing chemical handling and risk assessing SDS for hazardous materials (raw materials and batches). Integration of chemical controls and requirements into emergency plan –procedures \& identification of health surveillance requirements. 1\.3\. Collating chemical register and ensuring SDS are available and in the system for all products onsite. 1\.4\. Review and develop safety documents for assigned tasks including Policies, SOPs, forms and internal audit check sheets and reviews. 1\.5\. Prepare and or review process risk assessments of site operations. 1\.6\. Assist with employee work\-related injury investigation and ensure that the corrective action is completed. 1\.7\. Assist with the delivery of new starter inductions and contractor induction training 1\.8\. Assist with the continual development and roll out of H\&S training to site personnel. 1\.9\. Promote a positive safety culture. 2\. Perform safety inspections and audits and behavioral observations of work areas, documentation and files. 3\. Assist in organizing and completing fire and emergency preparedness drills. 4\. Maintain and co\-ordinate PPE inventory, issue and stocks management. 5\. Maintain and review day\-to\-day safety records including first aid supplies, emergency check sheets and contractors risk management systems. 6\. Monitor, analyse, and report on H\&S performance metrics, highlighting trends and improvement opportunities. 7\. Co\-ordination of safety training programme: (mandatory training) sourcing suppliers, co\-ordination training, managing records. 8\. Co\-ordinating PPE issue and inventory. 9\. Managing first aid provisions and systems. 10\. Determine appropriate and effective safety and compliance signage, bulletins and safety updates as required. 11\. Review contractor’s safety induction, risk assessments and safety compliance 12\. Maintain familiarity with company policies and procedures pertaining to Quality Manual, Food Security/Food Defense, Sanitation, cGMP's, and HACCP/Food Safety. 13\. Other duties and special projects as assigned. ***This list is for guidance only and the post holder will be required to undertake additional duties in line with the changing needs of the business.*** Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have a degree in Health \& Safety or a related field? Work Location: In person

title: Help Desk Coordinator
description: **Company Information and Introduction:** Diploma Lifesciences Ireland and UK comprises three leading companies: Accuscience Ireland Ltd., Technopath Distribution Ltd., Alpha Laboratories, and Electramed Ltd. Our purpose is simple yet powerful: we make a difference in people’s lives. As specialised, value\-adding distribution businesses, we supply essential products and services across the Healthcare, Pharma, Life Sciences, and Food sectors in Ireland and the UK. Our focus spans three key areas—Medical, In Vitro Diagnostics (IVD), and Scientific—where we deliver innovative solutions to hospitals, laboratories, community care providers, and scientific industries. Together, we form Diploma Lifesciences Ireland and UK, part of the Lifesciences Sector of Diploma PLC, a FTSE 100 company. This gives us the unique advantage of combining local agility with global strength. With over 135 dedicated team members, we are building a purpose\-led, values\-driven culture that fosters high performance, inclusivity, and career development. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity Bronze by the Irish Centre for Diversity—recognising our ongoing commitment to creating inclusive workplaces where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive. **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** **Purpose of role** Reporting to the Helpdesk Team Leader the Service Support Coordinator works collaboratively to manage the Service function administration in a timely and efficient manner. They provide customer support services to the business, ensuring customer service issues are dealt with in a timely manner and within Service Levels. **Responsibilities** * Take responsibility for the effective organisation and management of day\-to\-day administrative tasks that arise within the Service function. * Ensure items are processed and closed according to company deadlines, standards, and operational requirements. * Manage transactional entries on different software packages to ensure all data and records are maintained on our systems ensuring weekly/monthly/ quarterly reports are accurate. * Provide support to the operations team on scheduling of work and jobs on a daily/weekly basis, as needed. * Collaborating and working with the entire Service team to consistently deliver customer service excellence. * General administration in the service dept and any other adhoc general office duties, as directed by your manager. **Key Skills and Competencies:*** The ability to work independently and as part of a team, to multitask and to prioritise work * The ability to adapt to new tasks and flexibility in approach to work * Ability to multitask * Microsoft Outlook, Microsoft Office * Work Scheduling * Good communicator, comfortable dealing with people at all levels of the organization * Keen interest in developing expertise

title: IT & Continuous Improvement Assistant
description: **Reports to:** Continuous Improvement Lead **Job Purpose:** Support the day\-to\-day IT needs of the factory while assisting with continuous improvement, digitalisation, training and process standardisation projects **Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to:** **Basic IT Tasks:** * Provide first\-line support for IT\-related issues within the factory environment * Troubleshoot hardware, software, printer, scanner, mobile device and basis network issues * Escalate complex issues to Group IT or external providers where required * Support users with Microsoft 365, shared drives, factory systems and standard business applications * Assist with routine IT checks, updates and maintenance activities in line with company procedures and Group IT guidance * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems **Continuous Improvement \& Digitalisation Support:** * Support continuous improvement projects across production, operations, quality, engineering and training * Assist with process mapping, data collection, problem solving and action tracking * Help identify opportunities to reduce manual administration, improve information flow and standardise ways of working * Support the development of simple digital tools, forms, trackers, reports and dashboards * Assist with the rollout of factory improvement projects, ensuring users are trained and supported **Operational Training:** * Assist in operational training sessions for new starters, providing guidance and support as needed to facilitate smooth onboarding processes **IT Asset Management:** * Maintain an accurate inventory of all IT assets, including hardware, software licenses, and peripherals * Track the procurement, deployment, and retirement of IT assets according to established procedures and budgetary constraints * Conduct periodic audits to verify the physical location and condition of IT assets, reconciling discrepancies as necessary * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems * Professional development is encouraged to support career growth and progression **Qualifications/ Skills/ Experience:** **Essential:** * Demonstrated proficiency in basic IT tasks including hardware and software troubleshooting, and system maintenance. Ideally 1 year experience in IT environment * Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple responsibilities simultaneously * Show initiative and self\-motivation * Demonstrate a strong aptitude for problem\-solving * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively convey technical information and collaborate with cross\-functional teams * Proven ability to identify, analyse, and resolve IT\-related issues in a timely and efficient manner, minimising disruption to operations **Desirable:** * Experience in a manufacturing, food production or regulated environment * Familiarity with continuous improvement tools such as 5S, Lean, root cause analysis, standard work or process mapping * Experience with SharePoint, Power Apps, Power BI, Excel reporting or similar digital tools * Experience creating training materials, SOP’s videos or presentations * Knowledge of IT asset management, inventory control or software licence tracking * Understanding of basic IT security practices * Experience supporting system rollouts, user training or change management * Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) may be considered, based on relevant experience, training, or voluntary work Pay: €35,500\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience providing first\-line IT support to end users? * What Microsoft applications and systems have you used regularly? * Have you been involved in any process improvement or continuous improvement projects? * Are you comfortable providing hands\-on support on a factory floor, including troubleshooting PCs, printers, scanners, tablets, and user issues? Work Location: In person

title: IT Co-Ordinator
description: Glen Dimplex is a privately owned, international group headquartered in Ireland which operates across four main product areas: Heating \& Ventilation, Consumer Appliances, Flame and Precision Cooling. Glen Dimplex is one of the world's leading manufacturers of electric heating appliances and is a global leader in the development and delivery of low carbon solutions for space and water heating, cooling, and ventilation. Our purpose is to be a leader in the transition to a more sustainable world. This is underpinned by our ‘Empower Now’ Sustainability strategy. On our sustainability journey, we will listen to our stakeholders, learn about new processes and technologies, partner with experts that will challenge us, empower our staff to go further, and set long\-term goals that are embedded into our business plans. Details of brands and our can be found on our website. **ROLE OVERVIEW \- IT COORDINATOR** Glen Dimplex is seeking a highly organised and proactive IT Co\-ordinator to join our technology team. Reporting directly to the CTO, this role serves as a crucial operational support function within the IT department, ensuring smooth day\-to\-day operations and enabling the CTO and wider IT leadership team to focus on strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate will be an exceptional communicator with strong organisational skills, have the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment, with a keen interest in leveraging Office365 and modern Microsoft technologies including Power BI, Copilot, and Viva Engage to drive efficiency and innovation. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** **Adoption Platform** **IT Digital content platform for end\-user training and adoption** * Take ownership of the platform and become the subject\-matter expert, creating content and enabling others in IT to do the same * Support Community of Practice owners in developing and updating content * Organise the structure and approach for how we share and develop content * Own the product roadmap, working with the supplier to ensure required features are incorporated **Supplier \& Contract Management** * Set up new IT suppliers, including onboarding, account setup, and establishing working relationships * Organise and manage IT supplier contracts, ensuring compliance with company policies and renewal timelines * Maintain comprehensive records of all vendor agreements, SLAs, and contract terms * Raise and process purchase orders for IT equipment, software licenses, and services * Liaise with suppliers to resolve issues, negotiate terms, and ensure service delivery meets expectations * Track IT expenditure and maintain budget documentation **Meeting \& Workshop Facilitation** * Organize and facilitate IT leadership meetings and workshops * Coordinate logistics including room bookings, technology setup, catering, and materials * Prepare meeting agendas, distribute materials in advance, and capture accurate minutes * Track action items and follow up on deliverables to ensure accountability * Schedule and coordinate cross\-functional meetings with other departments **Business Communications \& Stakeholder Management** * Develop communication plans for providing notifications and updates to the business regarding technology initiatives * Communicate IT updates, system changes, and planned maintenance to business customers across the organization * Develop clear, user\-friendly communications that translate technical information for non\-technical audiences * Manage communication channels, including email, Viva Engage, Teams, and collaboration platforms, to ensure timely information dissemination **Presentation \& Documentation** * Create professional presentations for internal stakeholders, executive leadership, and external audiences (PowerPoint, Copilot, and draw.io) * Develop clear and compelling visual materials that communicate technical concepts effectively * Maintain and update IT documentation, policies, and procedural guidelines * Produce reports and dashboards on IT metrics, project status, and departmental performance **Executive \& Administrative Support** * Provide dedicated support to the CTO, including diary management, meeting scheduling, and priority management * Coordinate internal and external appointments, ensuring optimal use of the CTO’s time * Prepare briefing materials and meeting agendas * Handle correspondence and communications on behalf of the CTO when appropriate **Technology \& Innovation** * Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards to provide insights on IT operations, budgets, and project status * Create and deploy Power Apps solutions to streamline departmental workflows and processes * Leverage Microsoft Copilot to enhance productivity in documentation, communication, and data analysis * Stay current with emerging Microsoft 365 tools and identify opportunities to apply them within the IT department * Champion the adoption of low\-code/no\-code solutions to automate routine tasks **REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS \& SKILLS** **Essential:** * Proven experience in an administrative, coordination, or support role, preferably within an IT or technical environment * Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities simultaneously * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and collaboration tools * Keen interest and enthusiasm for learning and working with Microsoft 365, including Power BI, Power Apps, Microsoft Copilot, and Viva Engage * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to tailor messages for different audiences * High attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to handle confidential information with discretion and professionalism * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build relationships across all levels of the organization * Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and take initiative * Passion for technology and continuous learning **Desirable:** * Hands\-on experience with Power BI, Power Apps, Viva Engage, or other Microsoft Power Platform and Microsoft 365 tools * Understanding of IT terminology and systems * Experience with contract management or procurement processes * Experience supporting C\-level executives and directors * Experience with data visualization and reporting tools * Background in internal communications or stakeholder engagement * Experience developing communication strategies or plans * Familiarity with enterprise social networking and employee engagement platforms **PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES** * Proactive and solutions\-oriented mindset * Professional demeanour with strong business acumen * Adaptable and comfortable working in a dynamic environment * Strong problem\-solving abilities * Team player with a collaborative approach * Commitment to continuous improvement and learning * Curious and eager to explore new technologies and tools * Customer\-focused with strong service orientation **OUR VALUES AND CULTURE AND BENEFITS:** The Glen Dimplex values are important guiding principles and define the way all employees across the Group work: We Think Customer, We Care About People, We Value Innovation and We Keep It Simple. Our core values are ingrained in our DNA and play an active part in everything we do. Each one reminds us to stay true to ourselves whilst driving us to create innovative products and solutions for our customers. At Glen Dimplex, we are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all. We are focused on creating and inclusive culture and believe that it is essential to creating a dynamic and supportive workplace. We are committed to fostering a culture that embraces and celebrates differences in race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, and ability. We believe diverse perspectives and experiences are essential to our success as a company and a team. We believe in investing in our employees' well\-being and recognise the importance of work\-life balance. We offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees' physical, mental, and financial health. Our benefits package includes: * Competitive salary * Generous annual leave allowance * Private Health Insurance * Pension and Death in Service Benefit * Employee Assistance Programme * Permanent Health Insurance * Life Assurance * Supportive Family Leave policies. **We also offer additional benefits such as** * \-Generous discounts on Company products. * \-Bike to Work / Tax Saver initiatives. * \-Regular wellbeing talks supports and resources**.** By submitting your application, you agree that Glen Dimplex may collect your personal data for recruiting and related purposes. Glen Dimplex Privacy Policy explains what personal information we may process, where we may process your personal information, our purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Glen Dimplex's use of your personal information.

title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in **South County Dublin** is hiring a **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer** to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around **2–3 years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment** . MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a **5 days per week office\-based role** . This is non\-negotiable. You must already be **living in South County Dublin** , and you must be **fully legal to work in Ireland now** . There is **no visa sponsorship and no relocation support** for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **2–3 years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary for this Level 1 Technical Support Engineer role ranges from €35,000 \- €39,000 depending on your experience. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: IT Systems Administrator
description: **IT Systems Administrator** * Dublin 2, located close to Grand Canal dock station * Excellent salary \& benefits package; pension, healthcare \& bonus * Hybrid working arrangements A well\-established professional services organisation is seeking an experienced IT Systems Administrator to join its growing internal technology team. This is an excellent opportunity to work across modern infrastructure, cloud technologies, networking, and security within a collaborative and fast\-paced environment. **Responsibilities:** * Managing and supporting Windows Server and Microsoft 365 environments * Administering Active Directory, Azure AD, DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy * Supporting Exchange Online, Intune, SharePoint, Teams, and related services * Monitoring infrastructure performance and resolving technical issues proactively * Supporting networking technologies including Cisco and Meraki environments * Assisting with cybersecurity, patching, firewalls, VPNs, and compliance initiatives * Acting as an escalation point for technical support queries * Working closely with third\-party vendors and internal stakeholders **Requirements:** * Strong experience in Systems Administration within enterprise environments * Expertise across Windows Server, Microsoft 365, and virtualised infrastructure * Solid networking knowledge including switches, routers, VPNs, and wireless solutions * Experience with security tooling and infrastructure best practices * Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to manage competing priorities * Experience with SQL Server and enterprise applications is beneficial * Exposure to professional services environments is an advantage Apply below or reach out with any questions!

title: IT/OT Telecoms Network Architect
description: We are the people behind Ireland’s electricity grid. We operate the power system with world\-leading levels of variable asynchronous renewable generation, ensuring that everyone has power when they need it. But that’s not all — we are now transforming the power system for future generations. Our purpose is clear: to enable society to reduce carbon emissions and achieve climate change and net\-zero ambitions. This transformation requires innovation, resilience, and leadership in designing and deploying next\-generation OT and IT architectures that underpin critical infrastructure. **The Team** EirGrid Group has a unique role to play in leading this radical transformation of our power system in support of the transition to renewable energy. This will require pioneering engineering effort together with enhanced IT capability to respond successfully to the scale of disruption. This offers a unique opportunity to further your career in Telecom and Network Architecture while playing a significant role in delivering EirGrid Group’s Infrastructure transformation. We’re a Networking intensive business, and we operate a state\-of\-the\-art, specialised business\-critical systems, 24/7\. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an experienced **Network Architect** to join our growing team and play a key role in shaping the future of Ireland’s energy infrastructure. As we transition to a low\-carbon energy future, this is a unique opportunity to operate at a strategic level, influencing network architecture for both current operations and future requirements, including new interconnector projects and offshore renewable deployments. The Network Architect will be pivotal in designing, implementing, and governing the telecommunications and network architecture that underpins EirGrid’s critical operations. With ambitious plans to deploy offshore renewable energy generators over the next five years, there is an urgent need to enhance Network capabilities to support telemetry, SCADA, and Corporate assets and associated services for these installations. **Key Responsibilities*** Define and maintain the OT/IT network architecture roadmap, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy and regulatory requirements. * Lead the design and deployment of secure, high\-performance network solutions for critical infrastructure, including offshore renewable projects and interconnectors. * Oversee integration of OT and IT systems, ensuring interoperability, resilience, and compliance with cybersecurity standards. * Collaborate with offshore teams and external partners to deliver consistent architecture standards and accelerate project timelines. * Provide technical leadership and governance across network design, implementation, and operational support. * Develop and enforce architecture principles, policies, and best practices, including automation and standardisation. * Support business continuity and disaster recovery planning for network\-related services. * Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, influencing decisions on technology investments and solution approaches. **About You*** A recognised qualification in a relevant technical discipline supported by 10 years’ experience in senior network engineering or architecture roles within critical infrastructure environments. * Expert Knowledge of CISCO product suite Nexus, Catalyst, especially switching and routing * Proven expertise in OT networking, routing, switching, and WAN technologies (including MPLS) Port channels, VPC’s, Physical infrastructure. * Strong knowledge of network security, vulnerability management, and remediation. * Experience in architecting and delivering complex network solutions, including cloud and hybrid environments. * Demonstrated ability to develop operational procedures and run books, with a focus on automation. * Excellent technical and analytical skills, with the ability to operate effectively in complex environments. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams. Desirable * Professional certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, Azure Network Engineer Associate, or equivalent. * Experience with network automation tools and scripting (e.g., Python, Ansible, Netbox, OpsManage\+). **Closing date for applications** 08/06/2026 Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience. We value potential as much as experience**.** If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Inbound Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** ‘ARE YOU 'CUSTOMER FOCUSED \& TEAM PLAYER' **We Like That About You** Sandvik is a global, high\-tech engineering group, providing front\-line products and services for the mining and construction industries. The Sandvik Group has been in operation since 1862 and today employs some 40,000 people, working in approximately 160 countries. Our diverse solutions delivered by our highly qualified and proficient employees enhance our customers’ productivity, safety and their profitability. **We are now recruiting for an Inbound Specialist \- for a permanent role in our Dublin Office.** **Job Purpose** You manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio and ensure agreed performance targets are met, including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy. You proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions. You also conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required. You ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. End to end responsibility including PO placement, PO tracking from order to receipt, expediting urgent orders, resolving invoice discrepancies, data integrity maintenance and responding to sales region queries. You are also responsible for system parameter maintenance including lead\-time, safety stock levels and classification. You improve availability and reduce lead\-time through accurate and timely forecasting. **Main Responsibilities** **Key performance areas** * Day to day management of operational buying activities and driving supplier performance for the respective supplier base with focus on parts availability and working capital. * Expectations focus on making and implementing sound business decisions based on available data. * High level of collaboration with internal stakeholders including: Sourcing, Engineering and Customer Service. **Key Activities** * Manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio * Ensure agreed performance targets are met including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy * Proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions * Conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required * Ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. title: Inbound Transport Coordinator
description: **About Keelings** Keelings is a 100% Irish\-owned family business passionate about fresh produce. Operating across Ireland, the UK, and Europe, we grow, source, and supply top\-quality fruit and vegetables. Our success is built on innovation, sustainability, and strong partnerships \- powered by our dedicated people. **Purpose of the Role** Responsible for the day\-to\-day coordination and execution of inbound transport operations across multiple international supply routes and transport modes. Operating within a fast\-paced environment, the role supports the efficient, compliant and cost\-effective movement of products from global suppliers through to the business distribution network. The successful candidate will work closely with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents, shipping partners and internal departments to ensure inbound deliveries are planned, tracked and managed effectively. The role requires strong organisational skills, attention to detail and the ability to respond quickly to operational issues in order to maintain continuity of supply and service standards. This position plays an important role in supporting inbound transport operations, monitoring logistics performance, maintaining accurate records and ensuring compliance with customs, legal and company requirements across Ireland, the UK, EU and global import markets. The role will contribute to a culture of teamwork, accountability and continuous improvement while building strong working relationships with logistics partners and internal stakeholders. **Main Responsibilities** **Inbound Logistics Coordination** * Coordinate daily inbound logistics activities across road, sea and air freight operations. * Support the planning and scheduling of inbound deliveries in line with operational requirements and product shelf\-life considerations. * Monitor inbound shipments and proactively communicate delays, disruptions or service issues to relevant stakeholders. * Liaise with suppliers, hauliers, shipping lines and freight forwarders to ensure timely collection and delivery of products. * Assist with container scheduling, haulage planning and transport coordination to support daily operational targets. * Track supplier delivery performance and escalate issues where necessary. * Support customs clearance activities by coordinating documentation with customs agents and logistics partners. * Ensure all freight documentation, transport records and logistics systems are accurately updated within required timelines. * Assist with coordinating global imports by air and sea, ensuring all required documentation is completed correctly. * Maintain strong working relationships with third\-party logistics providers and transport partners. * Support the monitoring of inbound transport costs and assist with cost control initiatives. **Operational Support** * Work closely with the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director and wider logistics team to support smooth day\-to\-day operations. * Collaborate with Procurement, Sales, Warehouse, Technical and Planning teams to ensure efficient supply chain coordination. * Act as a key point of contact for inbound transport queries and operational updates. * Support logistics planning activities during peak trading periods and seasonal demand fluctuations. * Assist in identifying opportunities for process improvements and operational efficiencies. * Ensure all logistics activities are carried out in accordance with company Health \& Safety standards and operational procedures. * Support onboarding and training activities for new team members where required. **Compliance \& Administration** * Ensure compliance with customs procedures, transport regulations and approved haulier policies. * Assist in monitoring customs entry accuracy and resolving customs\-related queries. * Maintain accurate operational records and support KPI reporting for inbound logistics activities. * Complete daily and weekly administration tasks including shipment tracking, reporting and system updates. * Assist with invoice queries, transport cost reconciliations and supplier documentation checks. * Support supplier performance monitoring through KPI tracking and service reviews. * Ensure all required compliance and training activities are completed within designated timelines. **Key Accountabilities** * Coordination of inbound deliveries to ensure products arrive on time and in full. * Accurate maintenance of transport records, shipment documentation and logistics systems. * Effective communication with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents and internal stakeholders. * Support delivery of inbound transport service levels and operational targets. * Timely escalation and resolution of inbound logistics issues. * Maintenance of customs compliance and transport administration standards. * Contribution to continuous improvement initiatives within the logistics function. * Delivery of accurate reporting and operational support to the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director. **Skills \& Experience** * Previous experience in logistics, transport coordination or supply chain administration, ideally within fresh produce, FMCG or a fast\-paced environment. * Understanding of international freight operations across road, sea and air freight. * Working knowledge of customs procedures and import/export documentation would be advantageous. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. * Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving ability. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced and constantly changing environment. * Good commercial awareness and understanding of logistics operations. * Strong IT and systems skills, including Microsoft Office and transport/logistics systems. * Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team. **Special Circumstances** * Flexibility may be required to work outside normal business hours in line with operational requirements. * Participation in an on\-call rota required to support operational issues. * Role operates on a 5 days over 7 working pattern. * Occasional travel may be required depending on operational needs. **Education \& Qualifications** * Degree, diploma or relevant experience in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business or a related discipline. * Experience in transport coordination, logistics administration or supply chain operations. * Knowledge of customs clearance processes and Incoterms would be advantageous. * Understanding of inbound logistics and international freight operations preferred. **Benefits:** * Staff discount at Keelings Farm Shop and subsidized canteen * Free On\-site parking with access to electric chargers * Wellness Initiatives such as wellness week **Keelings Values** * People Matter * Teamwork * Integrity * Passion for Achievement **Inclusion \& Diversity Statement** **In Keelings we value all perspectives and knowledge that our colleagues bring to our business. We are committed to forming a diverse and inclusive team where all can share their unique experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all cultures and backgrounds and are dedicated to forming an environment of inclusiveness and diversity where all can thoroughly feel a sense of belonging.** **Reasonable Accommodation Statement** **If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please let us know at** recruitment\_keelings@keelings.com **Recruitment Process** * 1\. CV Submission \& email acknowledgment of receipt * 2\. Phone Screening * 3\. 1st Interview * 4\. 2nd Interview * 5\. Outcome \& Feedback *Keelings Careers \-* *https://www.keelings.com/careers\-keelings/* *Keelings Strategy \& Values \-* *https://www.keelings.com/our\-people/*

title: Information Technology Cross-Function Assistant Vice President
description: 4 Jun 2026 Dublin, D, IE, D02 KF20 **Who we are** -------------- At SMBC Group EMEA, we are united by a shared purpose: to be a trusted long‑term partner to our clients. As part of our expanding EMEA platform, we provide a full suite of corporate finance, investment banking, advisory, and global markets services. With award‑winning products and a universal banking model, we offer opportunities to work on impactful, diverse, and strategic initiatives that support our clients and the bank’s long‑term transformation. Sumitomo Mitsui Finance Dublin Limited (SMFD) which is a wholly owned subsidiary of SMBC and is growing rapidly as a Centre of Excellence for the bank’s universal banking business across EMEA. It provides a range of high\-quality technology and operational and control support services, aligned to SMBC’s growth, innovation, and transformation strategies. The role will be part of a dynamic, diverse team located in the heart of Dublin’s historic Georgian business district.**Role Summary** ---------------- We are seeking an IT Technical Lead for E\-Invoicing Platform (AVP) to manage the EMEA rollout for e\-invoicing platform (PAGERO) within the Information Technology, Security Department (ITSD), Cross Product Platforms group. You will lead the design, development, architectural integration and evolution, maintenance and delivery of e\-invoicing platform across SMBC EMEA entities. This is a unique opportunity to partner with technical delivery team to implement the regulatory compliance solution, service ownership and operational excellence of integrating invoice solutions through a strategic architecture, driving transformation and influencing how SMBC supports Operations, Finance and Tax functions. The role requires strong technical expertise, and service ownership to mature this capability for EMEA region, ensuring platform stability, continuous improvement and standardisation to enhance data quality, controls and alignment with global best practices. The role also encompasses system development, major enhancements, and strategic upgrades.**Position Description** ------------------------ * Product Strategy \& Roadmap: Define and implement strategy, roadmap planning, and architectural direction for rolling out e\-invoice services across SMBC EMEA entities. * Backlog Management: Own, and refine the product backlog, prioritise technical and customer facing requirements. * Service Ownership: Owning the end‑to‑end delivery lifecycle covering solution design, development oversight, system testing, and controlled implementation of e\-invoicing platform. * System Integration: Enable the integration of e\-invoicing platform – PAGERO, with multiple invoice processing systems, including COUPA, Accounts Payable (ERP System) and migrating some in\-house built invoice applications to the strategic platform. * Stakeholder Alignment: Collaborate with stakeholders across Finance, Tax, Operations, Regulatory Reporting to understand requirements, prioritise needs, and optimise invoicing solutions * Vendor Management: Manage vendor relationships, contracts and escalations effectively * Establish standardised operating models, governance controls, and quality frameworks across EMEA entities for invoice processing. * Monitor platform performance, risk, compliance, and audit findings, and leading the implementation of continuous improvements, remediation actions, and control uplifts **Required Experience** ----------------------- * Domain Knowledge: Good understanding of Order\-to\-cash (O2C) processes, including experience designing integrations between SaaS platforms and ERP / Finance systems (Accounts payable, Accounts Receivable) and billing. Ideally hands\-on experience with e\-invoicing SaaS platforms (e.g. PAGERO, COUPA etc.) * Experience operating as a product owner in technology or a change lead accountable for delivery, funded roadmaps and long\-term technology assets. * Agile Experience: Proven track record of working in agile/scrum methodologies, managing sprints and evaluating development progress with developers, QA and architects. * Delivery Management: Confident delivering at pace, across BAU, change delivery, testing cycles, and incident management * Strong communication, analytical and decision‑making skills, with a focus on structured problem‑solving and control improvement **Desirable Experience \& Education and Qualifications** -------------------------------------------------------- * Experience working in a hybrid business/technology domain * Degree‑level education in Information Technology, Computer Science, Finance, Business Information Systems or a related field (or equivalent professional experience) * Professional certifications such as ITIL, Agile/Scrum, or data management qualifications are advantageous but not essential **Competencies** ---------------- Customer Focus Driving Change Driving Results Embraces Diversity Enterprise Leadership Judgement and Decision Making Risk Management Strategic and Visionary Trust and Integrity**What we offer** ----------------- For all the expertise and experience you bring to help us make a difference, it’s only sustainable if we support you to build your career and be your best self so we offer competitive company benefits, centred around promoting positive well\-bring and work\-life balance. We also believe in fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, where all team members perspectives and contributions are valued. Initiatives in place which promote a diverse and inclusive culture and healthy work life balance include hybrid working, Sport \& Social Clubs and Diversity and Inclusion networks. As an employee of SMBC you have access to a host of both useful and exciting benefit offerings. service models. * Maintain continuous professional development and participate in performance management processes. * Support organisational quality customer service commitments. * Comply fully with title: Join the Jentic Talent Community
description: **Description** **About Jentic** At Jentic, we’re on a mission to build the core integration layer for AI, enabling developers to connect their AI systems to the world’s APIs easily and securely. We’re creating a critical missing piece of the future AI tech stack — middleware designed from the ground up for the AI era. We’re not just building an AI\-facing product, but an AI\-native company. Built by developers, for developers, our platform simplifies and accelerates API integration, increasing AI agent reliability, while solving security and access control challenges. **Requirements** **About Our Jentic Talent Community** Thank you for your interest in Jentic. We may not have a position right now that currently matches to your experience and expertise, however we would love if you shared your CV with us so we can get in touch with you about future roles when the time is right. It means that you'll be in our talent list so that when the right role comes up, we are able to contact you. **Benefits** **Why Join Us Now?** * Be a pioneer: Join an exceptionally experienced founding team at a critical stage in Jentic’s journey. Early team members will play a foundational role in the company’s growth and direction. * Mark your mark: Help define the core architecture of the future AI stack and make your mark during this unprecedented platform shift to AI. * Developer\-focused mission: Build tools that solve real\-world problems developers face when integrating AI agents. * AI\-native culture: Learn how to leverage AI to multiply your effectiveness and help create an organisation that reflects what businesses will look like in the years to come. **Take a First Class Ticket on the AI Train** This is your chance to work on a critical missing piece of the future of AI in a startup led by seasoned founders with a track record of success. Join us in building the essential middleware for AI, and help shape the future of software during the most transformative shift in decades. Ready to get on board? Apply now and let’s build the future together. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** Jentic is an equal opportunity employer, and we value diversity in the workplace. We are committed to providing an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates. We don't just tolerate differences—we celebrate them. Our strength comes from perspectives that challenge the status quo.

support during contract negotiations. * Manage contract\-related data, including contract management software or databases. * Assist with invoicing, payment processing, and other financial tasks other duties as may be required or assigned as part of the role. **Experience** * 3 years’ experience in a contract administration support role in a high value rail projects or projects of a similar scale / complexity. * Experience in assisting with the drafting, reviewing, and administration of commercial contracts. * Experience dealing with a range of stakeholders at all levels across the organisation. **Skills** * Good influencing skills to manage and motivate teams in support of delivery of high risk, value and complex project and programmes. * Ability to coordinate and organise multiple sources of reporting inputs / performance data and deliver project / programme reports as required. * An ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills to facilitate clear and consistent dialogue between all parties involved. * Ability to govern and drive outcomes to ensure high quality service. * Comprehensive knowledge of cost management principles, methods, techniques and tools, including the standard method of measurement. **Qualifications** * C Eng / BEng / BA / BSc cost management, quantity surveying, estimating 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and title: Junior Electrical Design Engineer
description: Anord Mardix, a Flex company, is a global leader in critical power solutions. With facilities around the globe, Anord Mardix leads in critical power infrastructure, supporting a myriad of industries, from financial institutions to data centres. At Anord Mardix, we welcome people of all backgrounds. We develop skill sets, encourage curiosity, and enable empowerment – we turn jobs into careers. If this sounds interesting, we’d like to meet you. To support our extraordinary teams who build great products and contribute to our growth, we’re looking to add a **Junior Electrical Design Engineer** located in **Dundalk, Ireland.** This role will provide excellent experience in the design and development of electrical switchgear and associated products. We are looking for a graduate level design engineer with an eye for detail who can work independently and as part of a team. Good interpersonal and communication skills are a must, as the successful candidate will work with multiple disciplines during products lifecycle. **What a typical day looks like:** * Under the direction and mentorship of senior members of the design team, carry out the electrical design of switchgear and other critical power distribution products. * Create general arrangements and electrical schematics using AutoCAD and AutoCAD electrical * Generate accurate bills of materials for purchasing along with other supporting documentation such as work instructions * Maintain accurate \& up to date records of designs and documentation * Participate in providing technical support to internal and external customers, attending meetings where necessary * Work actively within the engineering team to help foster a positive and supportive working environment **The experience we’re looking to add to our team:** * Have a third level qualification in Electrical Engineering or come from a related technical background * Relevant experience in a switchgear production environment (electrical) will be considered in lieu of a third level qualification * Have a solid understanding of electricity fundamentals and electrical circuit design * Be proficient in the use of Microsoft Office tools * Have a methodical approach to problem solving * Understanding of electrical engineering drawings such as single line diagrams and schematics is advantageous **What you’ll receive for the great work you provide:** * An environment where you will feel fulfilled by your work, valued for your contribution, and celebrated for your success; * Opportunities to learn new skills in a fast\-paced industry; * A competitive salary and benefits package that includes: + A merit\-based annual pay review + Enhanced annual leave + Employee recognition scheme and long service awards + Referral bonus + Volunteer days + Group Life Insurance, including a death\-in\-service payment and access to a virtual GP service 24/7 365 days a year + Company Pension Scheme + Sick pay scheme + Cycle to Work scheme + Enhanced maternity/paternity leave * Flexible/Remote/Hybrid Work based on your Job Function; * Travel opportunities (role dependent); * Support your well\-being by access to: + Employee Assistance Programme through VHI offering free access to qualified counsellors and expert advice. + VHI Private Health Insurance + On\-site trained Mental Health First Aiders * Access to various discount programs (including food, activities, gym memberships etc.). TH10 Anord Mardix is an Equal Opportunity Employer and employment selection decisions are based on merit, qualifications, and abilities. We do not discriminate based on: age, race, religion, color, sex, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, disability, pregnancy status, or any other status protected by law. We're happy to provide reasonable accommodations to those with a disability for assistance in the recruitment process. Please advise us of any accommodations you may need by e\-mailing: accessibility@flex.com.

title: Junior Java Developer
description: **Junior Java Developer (1\- 4 year of experience )** **Initial 12 month contract** **Dublin city center** Our high profile client based in Dublin city center is actively seeking an Junior Java Developer(1\- 4 years of experience) with Red Hat OpenShift, Hat PAM, Red Hat Single Sign\-On, Keycloak, TypeScript, Node JS experience for an initial 12 month contract. **Key Deliverables** The following non\-exhaustive list of Key Deliverables are applicable to this Role. * Programming of Java/j2EE components * Completing assigned tasks related to phases of development lifecycle of enterprise applications within given scope and timeframe requirements. * Coding in adherence to the standards of the application in question * Maintaining, tuning and repairing applications in order to keep them performing according to technical and functional specifications. * Performing preventive maintenance activities. * Providing user support. * Integration of technical and application components to meet business requirements. * Writing and execution of unit and integration test plans * Incident resolution * Ensure production releases are compliant with client release management standards. * Conducting development code reviews to ensure adherence to JAVA coding standards. * Organisation of skill/knowledge transfer and mentoring of team members to staff. * Work with other Scrum teams to increase the effectiveness of the application of Scrum in client. * Facilitate Scrum events as requested or needed. * Developers must be able to work on every stage of the software development life cycle from concept to design testing. The role will entail a combination of both technical and business\-related functions. **The following Experience/Competencies/Skillsets are applicable to this Role.** o Microservice and API development (RESTful, experience of REST design and implementation). o Understanding of API security protocols (OAuth 2\.0/0penID Connect). o Knowledge of Kubernetes, API managers and WebLogic. o Experience with OpenShift Container platform, 3scale API and KeyCloak. o Testing frameworks and TDD (like Cucumber or Junit). o Databases: Relational Oracle is preferred. o Integration of technical and application components to meet business requirements. A demonstrated exposure to some or all the following technologies: o Java (Enterprise) o Red Hat OpenShift, or similar, however Red Hat OpenShift is preferred. o Red Hat PAM, or similar, however Red Hat PAM is preferred. o Red Hat Single Sign\-On, or similar, however Red Hat SSO is preferred. o Keycloak, or similar, with Red Hat Keycloak preferred o Java Application servers, with Weblogic preferred o TypeScript o NodeJS o RESTful APIs o Git o Relational Database, with Oracle preferred o PL/SQL o HTML o CSS / SCSS o SQL Developer o UX/UI Development o Message Broker Technologies
