title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence (in our brand new office!) and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER - Dawson street
description: We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Here’s what we value most: * Love for Food – You care about flavour, freshness, and making food that looks as good as it tastes. * Knife Skills – You know your way around a chopping board (or you’re eager to learn). Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Kitchen Assistant/Kitchen Porter
description: **Job Opportunity:** kitchen assistant/ kitchen porter **at Ladurée Ireland** Ladurée Ireland is looking for dedicated kitchenassistant **to** join our team! Are you organized, and a team player who thrives under pressure? Then you might be exactly who we’re looking for. **What we’re looking for** * **Proven experience** in a similar role is ideal — but not essential * Someone who is **reliable**, **organized**, and **detail\-focused** * A positive team player who can handle busy environments with grace * Commitment to **maintaining a clean and efficient workspace** No experience With pastri? No problem! If you have the passion and the right attitude, we’re happy to provide full training. ( at least experience working in a kitchen is required) **If you are eager to learn and grow**, you're the person we want on our team! Come be part of a brand with a rich tradition and a taste for excellence. Job Type: Part\-time Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Linux Administrator
description: We are at the heart of Ireland’s energy transition \- connecting, operating and transforming the electricity grid to support a low carbon, renewable future. We are building talented teams that tackle real world challenges every day and help us to achieve our goal to prepare the grid to carry up to 80% of Ireland’s electricity from renewable sources. We are proud to be a certified Great Place to Work, recognised for our culture built on trust, collaboration and inclusion. At EirGrid you will find supportive teams, opportunities to grow and meaningful work. We also offer secure employment with a range of flexible and family friendly working policies to support your life and wellbeing. If you want to know more about what EirGrid has to offer you, visit Life at EirGrid. If you are ready to make an impact and grow your career in a purpose\-led organisation, your journey starts here. **The Opportunity** The Linux and Database team is well established within the IT Infrastructure Department and is responsible for the organisation’s extensive Linux, Oracle, PostGres and Microsoft SQL Server solutions. A particular focus of this team is to ensure the reliability of the various platforms whilst driving performance and functional improvements and new delivery mechanisms including virtualisation and cloud services. The Linux Administrator will assist in the development and support of EirGrid’s Linux estate on Physical and Virtual machines which underpin the organisation’s corporate functions and mission\-critical power system operation and market functions. The focus of the role will be to support major programs of work to upgrade and implement in premise and cloud\-based systems. The successful applicant will contribute to the overall success of Eirgrid by working as part of a team of IT professionals, undertaking Linux administration duties and driving project delivery and solution optimisation. **Key Responsibilities*** Support the Linux \& Database Team Lead in delivering on project targets and operational KPIs * Install, configure, and maintain Linux operating systems Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), Oracle Linux environments. * Monitor system performance, troubleshoot issues, and ensure high availability and reliability of Linux\-based infrastructure * Configure network interfaces (NICs), Ethernet devices, and manage ILO for HP servers * Manage storage, including SAN, NAS, RAID, multipathing, zoning, and LVM * Diagnose and troubleshoot software, hardware, and network issues to identify root causes * Manage package installations, updates, and maintain software inventories * Configure, monitor, and manage backups and disaster recovery plans * Ensure system uptime, compliance, and handle user access management * Develop and maintain automation scripts (Bash, Python) for routine tasks and monitoring * Handle change management processes, system upgrades, and patch deployments * Implement and manage OS firewalls, security configurations, and vulnerability management * Configure and troubleshoot virtualization environments (e.g., VMware). * Automate system administrative tasks using scripting languages such as Bash or Python to streamline processes and improve efficiency. * Evaluate and recommend hardware and software upgrades to improve system performance and scalability. * Provide in\-depth troubleshooting and issue resolution for the Red Hat and Oracle Linux ecosystem. * Investigate root causes of issues and escalate to engineering or vendor support when needed. * Support the asset management of Linux systems including inventory maintenance, warranty management, vendor support and configuration backups * Oversee the development of internal procedures, processes and documentation * Promote high standards of platform design, operation and availability and ensure that support capabilities during and outside normal working hours are maintained * Liaise with the EirGrid’s Governance \& Planning team and business representatives to understand business needs and the wider context of developing and delivering IT solutions * Provide effective stakeholder management of both internal and external stakeholders including strategic vendors and service providers * Proficiency in capacity planning and disaster recovery planning Provide training and mentoring to junior staff * **About You** The successful candidate will have: * An IT degree or recognised qualification in a relevant technical discipline * 7\+ years’ experience in delivering and/or operating critical business solutions * Proficiency in Red Hat and Oracle Linux OS environments * Expertise in networking protocols (TCP/IP, DHCP, DNS, VPN) and firewall management * Strong skills in SAN, NAS, RAID configurations, multipathing, and LVM * In\-depth experience with Bash and Python scripting for automation * Hands\-on experience with VMware and other virtualization technologies * Familiarity with TCP/IP, VLANs, load balancing, and high availability configurations * Installation \& configuration of Operating system, ILO * CPU Utilization, Memory Management * Certifications in Red Hat Certified System Administrator (RHCSA) or Red Hat Certified Engineer (RHCE) * Effective communicator, both in verbal and written communications supported by excellent customer facing skills * Successful track record in delivering IT projects * Strong analytical and problem\-solving capability **Closing date for applications** 19/06/2026 Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience. We value potential as much as experience**.** If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: M365 Consultant - Microsoft Partner of the Year
description: Current **Microsoft Partner of the Year** , Ergo, are seeking an **M365 Consultant** for an exciting role with our Modern Work team. The successful Cloud Architect will be responsible for providing expert consultancy, designing and implementing solutions, and ensuring the successful delivery of Microsoft 365 solutions to some of Ireland's most well\-known public and private sector companies. You will work on an **MVP\-led team** in a company awarded Microsoft Country **POTY SIX times** in addition to FIVE MS **Modern Workplace Partner of the Year** awards. title: Managing Consultant
description: *At Fruition Group Ireland we embrace our values of* ***Pride*** *,* ***Energy*** *and* ***Perseverance*** *and our colleagues embed them as the foundation of their work. Our colleagues are proud to work for Fruition Group, they are pro\-active with high energy to maintain our market\-leading status. With a diverse team we recognise that no two people are the same and we focus on results rather than unachievable KPIs and work collaboratively to achieve our goals whilst having fun along the way!* *We are a high growth organisation who work with a huge range of technology employers throughout the UK, Ireland, EU and USA and as a business we re\-invest profits to ensure we are leading in recruitment innovation in our marketplace and equipping our colleagues with the best environment, tools and training to help them succeed.* *Following the great success and growth for our team in Bray we are now opening a new office in Dublin City! This is a great opportunity to join a team on a* ***Managing*** *level with possibilities to lead and be part of a team with both current and new colleagues!* ***The role*** Reporting directly into the Directors, who are some of Irelands biggest billers. You will play a pivotal part in the resourcing of candidates for our clients and the development of new client relationships. You will continue to develop your network of candidates within the tech (perm and contract) space and continually keeping up to date with current trends. You will think outside of the box for sourcing strategies and utilise all internal tech provided to maximise efficiency. There is excellent opportunity to be part of a new office with a successful current business that is rapidly growing globally. Where you get the chance to develop your mentoring and leadership skills, as you will begin to support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment. ***Key Responsibilities*** * Development of accounts into key accounts * Searching for suitable Tech candidates who match our clients’ requirements * Headhunting and networking on sites such as LinkedIn / GitHub / Stack Overflow * Writing and posting job adverts * Telephone interviewing candidates to assess their suitability for specific roles * Arranging interviews for candidates and liaising with them during this time * Making job offers to suitable candidates and handling rejections * Taking references / collating relevant supporting documents * Building relationships with candidates to create a strong talent pipeline * Use social media to create a personal brand * Keep up to date with trends within the Tech industry * Building relationships with existing and future clients * Utilise internal tech such as SourceWhale, LinkedHelper and Paiger * Ensure all data is accurately recorded on CRM * Attending meeting with clients to explore potential opportunities * Negotiating fees and terms of engagement * Manage the team \& support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment ***Key Skills and Capabilities*** * Outstanding communicational skills with the ability to adapt language to different audiences * Ability to build long—lasting professional relationships * Ability to handle difficult conversations * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills, attention to detail; well organised * Proactive and independent thinker * Excellent customer service skills * Strong presentation skills with the ability to convey key messages to diverse and wide audiences * Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement skills * Experience working to targets * Great negotiating skills * Excellent Experience using the telephone in a professional environment is desirable * Great understanding of technical recruitment * Experience within a sales environment is desirable ***Your Benefits to name a few:*** * **Competitive basic salary with uncapped earning potential** * **Lucrative commission – no thresholds!** * **Ongoing training with external learning providers** * **Clear and structured Career progression** * **Biannual winners trip to European destinations such as Marbella, Barcelona \& Amsterdam** * **Flexible working offer \- between the office and home** * **Quarterly lunch clubs for top achievers** * **Quarterly team socials** * **Friday early finish** * **Private healthcare** * **Pension Contribution** If you’re interested in joining Fruition as Managing Consultant, please get in touch for an initial confidential chat about our available opportunities, and we’ll happily share our story with you. title: Mechanical Day Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Mechanical Day Technician** **Contract Duration/Type:** **Permanent** **Location:** **Clondalkin, Dublin** **Hours:** **Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30** **Overview of the role:** The Day Mechanical Technician will train on all Processes and techniques across Predictive Maintenance, CMMS (Pemac), Reliability techniques on Fuel Oil, industrial Gas distribution, Turbines, Boilers Compressors, Diesel Generators Pumps. The working hours are Monday\-Thursday 07:30 to 16:00 Friday 07:30 to 15:30 The day lead technician may be required from time to time to back fill on shift, and may also be required to support the site in emergency situations outside of normal working hours. **Duties of the Role include:** * Coordinate the day to day work with shift technicians on\-site particularly morning shift changeover * Be the point of contact for service providers * Trouble\-shooting and high\-level investigation of utility plant equipment faults and failures – root cause analysis * Compliance/quality engineering – recording and investigation of deviations on utility plant and equipment * Carry out planned preventive, predictive and corrective maintenance as required * Ensure PMs are completed on time * Good knowledge and understanding of CMMS systems * Develop PMs and Job Plans as well as SOPs for plant equipment. * Liaising with site planner re stores to identify spare part requirements; * Review maintenance programme periodically to ensure optimal uptime levels are achieved * Support and comply with all Safety procedures * Experience with: Fuel Oil storage and distribution systems. Industrial gas installations and distribution. Gas and Steam turbines, diesel generators, boilers * Be able to take a leadership role in decision\-making as regards to plant operation and equipment suitability for operation; * Have sufficient knowledge of the plant to allow decision making in terms of taking plant off line, communicating with relevant people on site and be capable of identifying the action required to get plant back on line or introduce back\-up and standby systems; * Understand the actions to be taken in the event of a serious service outage; * Provide leadership and mentoring to the team in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Provide leadership and mentoring to team members in developing their capability to resolve complex plant deviations and failures * Support the Site engineer \& HV Engineer in carrying out Root Cause Analysis on plant failures to determine the sequence of events, the underlying root cause and assist the in developing proposals to remedy the fault; * Provide support to capital improvement projects on utilities systems, and be involved in the commissioning and hand over from projects to operations. * Assist the Site Engineer \& HV Engineer in planning and executing complex utility service isolations for maintenance, projects and client works; * Identify, develop and, if approved, implement Continuous Improvement measures to improve the efficiency of plant and working practices; * Seek to continuously improve knowledge and understanding of complex mechanical systems; **Required skills for this role:** * Analytical mindset * Experience with six sigma/lean preferred * Previous experience required, craft qualified in a mechanical discipline * Past working experience ideally gained in energy conversion and distribution **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

requirements. * Produce report for the projects you are assigned to and issue to direct manager at agreed periods. * Assist with tenders for new projects when required. * Assisting the estimating dept and pe\-construction team at agreed periods * Assist with setting up the value engineering log for the DC\&BS group. * All other tasks related to Design role that is requested by your direct line projects, how to produce tech subs, manage drawing production, issuing of RFI’s, dealing with clients. * Attend projects within Europe for site visits from time to time. * Attend monthly Central Engineering F2F meeting. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Third level qualification in Mechanical Engineering/ Mechanical Design Engineering * 3\+ years experience working on large industrial plant projects * Proficiency in a related Mechanical Design package/ software * Excellent communication skills * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Must be able to work from our Dublin Headquarters **Desirable Criteria** * Experience working on datacentres would be an advantage but not essential Mercury is an equal opportunities employer. Apply Now

title: Mechanical Maintenance Technician
description: **Job Description :** **M****echanical****Maintenance****Engineer** **Littleconnell****Brewery,****Co.Kildare** **12\-month Fixed\-Term****Contract** **Closing date: 19/06/2026** **Mechanical****Maintenance****Engineer****\- €****73,710 (including****shift all****owance****)****plus****competitive benefits package with pension, shares scheme and bonus.** **Join our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team.** Our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team are problem\-solvers, sourcing, producing, and delivering our much\-loved brands to delight customers around the globe. Over half of Diageo’s 30,000\+ people work in Supply Chain and Manufacturing. It’s an intricate and sophisticated operation that’s the product of logistical, manufacturing, and technical collaborations. We are proud to be a global leader in beverage alcohol with an exceptional portfolio of brands, including Guinness, Johnnie Walker, Baileys, Smirnoff, Tanqueray, and many more. A forward\-thinking €200 million investment has brought an innovative, carbon\-neutral brewery to life in Newbridge, Co. Kildare. This best\-in\-class facility is Ireland’s second\-largest brewing operation and a powerhouse of sustainability, running entirely on renewable energy. It’s where iconic beers like Rockshore, Harp, Smithwick’s, and Kilkenny are crafted for a new generation. **About the Role** We are seeking a Mechanical Maintenance Engineer who will be responsible for ensuring the highest standards of maintenance and asset care across the brewery, utilities, and water treatment plants. Focusing on end\-to\-end maintenance support, you will play a crucial role in both operations and maintenance within a semi\-autonomous, shift\-based environment, managing SAP process orders and maintenance planning as part of your rotational duties. You will be instrumental in upholding our health and safety compliance standards, maintaining equipment and systems, ensuring asset availability and reliability to achieve high performance. In addition, you’ll be monitoring key performance indicators such as equipment efficiency. Your commitment to these areas will be vital in driving continuous improvement and supporting the success of the team. Key Responsibilities: * Work across key production areas such as raw materials handling, the brewhouse, the cold block, and utilities plants (water treatment, process utilities, and refrigeration) and support \& understand departmental KPI’s. * Uphold safety, quality, food safety, and environmental standards and procedures * Collaborate with different teams to effectively identify and resolve equipment\-related issues that impact performance and provide a comprehensive maintenance backup service for production activities. * Coach operators to perform autonomous maintenance tasks, machine operational setups, and support growth in equipment capability as required. Support capital work including installation, commissioning, testing within areas as required; responsible for feeding into early equipment management. * Ensure all aspects of work order planning are delivered at start\-of\-shift meetings, including spare parts planning for planned maintenance. * Oversight of issuing permits for external contractors. Understand and perform isolations for Diageo and contract personnel * Apply Manufacturing Excellence tools and techniques to drive site performance towards world class in terms of customer service and quality **About you** For this role you will be a time served Mechanical Technician and will have obtained a minimum of the Junior Certificate or equivalent. In addition, you’ll have the following: * Ability to organise a variety of tasks and prioritise self and others accordingly * Knowledge of PM and Reliability Maintenance techniques * Understanding of asset performance and maintenance strategy with excellent process improvement and root cause problem solving skills * Knowledge of SAP\-PM (Planning, scheduling and execution) * Basic financial awareness, understanding of business drivers, maintenance costs and budgeting processes. * FMCG experience is preferred but not essential We’re looking for someone who can work well within a team as well as the ability to make effective conclusions and thrive in situations where they can solve problems. **Working with us** Our purpose is to celebrate life, every day, everywhere. And creating an inclusive culture, where everyone feels valued and that they can belong, is a crucial part of this. We embrace diversity in the broadest possible sense. This means that you’ll be welcomed and celebrated for who you are just by being you. You’ll be part of and help build and champion an inclusive culture that celebrates people of different gender, ethnicity, ability, age, sexual orientation, social class, educational backgrounds, experiences, mindsets, and more. Our ambition is to create the best performing, most trusted and respected consumer products companies in the world. Join us and help transform our business as we take our brands to the next level and build new ones as part of shaping the next generation of celebrations for consumers around the world. Feel inspired? Then this may be the opportunity for you! *If you require a reasonable adjustment, please ensure that you capture this information when you**submit**your application.* **Worker Type :** Regular**Primary Location:** Littleconnell Brewery**Additional Locations :** **Job Posting Start Date :** 2026\-06\-04

description: **Company Description** **Who we are?** We are JDE Peet’s. We craft coffee for every cup and build brands for every heart. JDE Peet’s is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, serving approximately 4,400 cups of coffee per second in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focusing on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and Jacobs, alongside a collection of 9 local icons; such as Douwe Egberts, Kenco, Pilao, Old Town, Moccona, Super, Gevalia, Friele, Marcilla. In 2024, JDE Peet’s generated total sales of EUR 8\.8 billion and employed a global workforce of more than 21,000 employees. **What’s it like to work at JDE Peet’s?** We believe amazing things happen over a cup of coffee. Together, we create those moments every day, turning ideas into impact and possibilities into reality. At JDE Peet’s, we work as one team guided by our values: we Dare to Amaze, Own It, Make It Simple, and Win Together. Here, you’ll find the freedom to innovate, the courage to take bold steps, and the support to grow your career in a global environment. Come join our team and discover what your cup of amazing can be! Find out more at http://careers.jdepeets.com **Job Description** The National Account Executive supports the management and growth of key national customers across Ireland. The role focuses on supporting Account Managers, executing customer plans, analysing sales data, and ensuring excellence in promotional admin execution. This role is an incredible opportunity for anyone who has an interest in the FMCG industry. The successful candidate will have the chance to develop, improve, and hone highly transferable commercial skills applicable across industries and markets. Additional to the National Account Executive responsibilities, our candidate will have the opportunity to assist marketing and category management, gaining firsthand knowledge of these exciting roles. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the National Account managers with their day\-today tasks, including new product set up, running reports, creating presentations etc. * Prepare and submit promotional submissions in line with the customer calendars. * Create promotional briefs to share with the field sales team. * Manage promotional funding and forecasting in our internal platform XTEL. * Monitor customer pricing and amend any discrepancies in SAP. * Support Marketing with PR activity and budget management. * Assist Category Management with data analysis and creating presentations. **Qualifications** Working at JDE Peet’s is hands\-on, giving you the chance to leave your mark while working together as one team. We’re a fast\-paced company on a mission, focused on what matters most. Our environment demands energy, agility, and bold thinking. If you’re ready to make an impact and grow in a global team, JDE Peet’s is the place for you. For this role as a National Account Executive, we’re looking for someone who has the following skills and experience: * 1\-3 years’ experience in sales or a commercial role (FMCG experience highly valued). * Proficient with Microsoft Office programmes, particularly Excel. * Strong numerical and analytical skills with high attention to detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced commercial environment. * Full right to work/work permits for the duration of the contract **Additional Information** **Your application at JDE Peet's** If you are interested in this career opportunity at JDE Peet's, apply and join us. After receiving your application we will do our utmost to give you quick feedback. Part of the recruitment procedure at JDE Peet's would be an online assessment, followed by interviews. A pre\-employment check can be part of the recruitment process. **Our DE\&I Statement** At JDE Peet’s we aim to create a better future where we authentically serve, reflect and embrace everyone. We believe in fostering a more diverse, equitable and inclusive organisation where everyone feels comfortable to truly be who they are and unleash their full potential. This means we do not tolerate discrimination on the basis of our race, ethnicity, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, age, disability, or any other trait protected by law. Throughout the entire employee lifecycle, we place DE\&I at the core of our people processes. This enables us to drive objectivity and eliminate any potential bias, ensuring an inclusive experience in the workplace. Do not hesitate to reach out if you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. **Our efforts on this agenda have earned us the prestigious Catalyst Award, as well as the Fair Pay Leader Certification in 2025 thanks to an unexplained gender pay gap of 0\.52%.** *\#unleashyourpossibility \#acupofamazing* JDE Peet’s \- now part of Keurig Dr Pepper – is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, with a presence in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focused on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and our 10 strategically selected local icons led by Jacobs.

title: Network Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Senior Network Engineer to support and enhance enterprise network infrastructure within a large\-scale operational environment. This is a hands\-on contract role focused on firewall administration, network security operations, and infrastructure support across on\-premise and cloud environments. The successful candidate will play a key role in maintaining secure, resilient, and high\-performing network services. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and support of enterprise firewall platforms including technologies such as Fortinet, Check Point, Palo Alto, or Azure Firewall * Management of firewall policies, NAT, VPN connectivity (site\-to\-site and remote access), and security rulebases * Troubleshooting complex network, routing, and connectivity issues across LAN, WAN, and cloud environments * Support and maintenance of core network services including switching, routing, DNS, DHCP, and network segmentation * Implementation and support of network security controls in collaboration with cybersecurity teams * Participation in infrastructure upgrades, network changes, and implementation projects * Operational support for cloud networking environments, preferably Microsoft Azure * Maintenance of network documentation, configurations, and operational procedures * Knowledge transfer and collaboration with internal technical teams **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5–10\+ years’ experience in Network Engineering or Infrastructure Engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience managing enterprise firewall environments * Excellent troubleshooting and diagnostic capability across complex networks * Experience supporting enterprise LAN/WAN infrastructure * Strong understanding of networking protocols and security principles * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced operational environment **Desirable Experience** * Experience with Azure networking and cloud security services * Exposure to regulated or compliance\-driven environments * Familiarity with security frameworks and governance standards * Experience working within large enterprise IT operations teams **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines **Benefits:** * Staff Discount * Employee Assistance Programme * Day time hours Monday \- Friday 9am\-6pm * Cycle to Work Scheme * Pension Scheme

title: PhD Studentship
description: Trinity is Ireland’s leading university and is ranked 75th in the world (QS World University Rankings 2026\). Founded in 1592, the University is steeped in history with a reputation for excellence in education, research, and innovation, which has been inspiring generations of thinkers for over 400 years. Thrive: Trinity College Dublin's Strategic Plan 2025 \- 2030 The renovation and public closure of Trinity College Dublin’s (TCD) Long Room in the Old Library present an unparalleled opportunity to conduct pioneering research “on”, “through”, and “for” this iconic space. This initiative will redefine how we preserve and enhance one of Europe’s premier cultural heritage landmarks. This project will delve into aspects of the Long Room that have been largely overlooked: its acoustic experiences and sonic architecture. This project will use state\-of\-the\-art robot\-aided acoustic measurements and employ advanced computational and acoustic modelling techniques to create a versatile Acoustic Digital Twin, which will be combined with existing 3D image data. **Application Procedure** Application Deadline: 12pm (GMT), 26 June 2026 Applicants should submit a completed application package to: Name: Jennifer O’Meara, Associate Professor in Film Studies Email Address: jennifer.omeara@tcd.ie **Our Values \- putting our words into action** Values matter. They are key to the success of our Strategic Plan. We centre our behavioural values around the importance of *belonging*. We recognise that the university will thrive by each of us *respecting and caring* for one another. We know we can *achieve* much more through working *together*. We know too that we must *take responsibility* for our actions and be accountable. We must, and will never stop, *imagining*. And through *being nature positive* we connect not just with the environment but with our ambitions for a sustainable future. At Trinity, we are committed to equality, diversity, and inclusion. Trinity welcomes applications from all individuals, including those applicants with disabilities, those who may have had non\-traditional career paths, those who have taken time out for reasons including family or caring responsibilities. We also welcome international applicants including those who have been displaced due to war. Trinity College Dublin is Ireland’s premier University. We are an EU Sustainable Gender Equality Champion and we hold an Athena Swan Silver award, recognising our ongoing work to advance gender equality both within Trinity and in the Higher Education sector. Trinity is committed to supporting work\-life balance and to creating a family\-friendly working environment.

title: Pipe Fitter Apprentice Year 1
description: **The Role** * Due to continuous growth within the company, Murphy Ireland are currently looking for Apprentice Pipefitter(s) to join and contribute towards some new and exciting projects. * Please note that travel is required **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the fabrication, installation, maintenance, and repair of high\- and low\-pressure piping systems. * Support experienced pipefitters in measuring, cutting, prepping, threading, and assembly of pipe fabrications of varying sizes (from small\-bore to large\-diameter systems). * Help install piping systems that carry liquids, gases, and other materials in accordance with technical drawings and specifications. * Participate in welding, fabrication, and fitting activities under supervision. * Learn to interpret and follow engineering drawings, fabrication drawings, and isometric diagrams. * Use and maintain a range of hand tools, power tools, and specialised pipefitting equipment safely and effectively. * Assist in testing, inspecting, and troubleshooting piping systems to identify faults or leaks. * Support routine preventative maintenance activities to ensure system reliability and safety. * Maintain a high level of Health and Safety which includes the proper use of PPE. * Adhere to work instructions and procedures. * Meet daily, weekly and monthly targets. * Keep site and fabshop clean and safe to operate in. **Key Requirements** **Qualifications \& Skills:** - Must be eligible to register as a pipefitting apprentice with SOLAS - The minimum educational requirements are: * Grade D in five subjects in the Department of Education \& Skills Junior Certificate Examination or an approved equivalent, or 2\. * The successful completion of an approved Pre\-Apprenticeship course or 3\. * Three years’ work experience gained over sixteen years of age in a relevant designated industrial activity as SOLAS shall deem acceptable. * Safe Pass, Manual Handling and Abrasive Wheels * Leaving Certificate with O6 – O1 in Maths (desirable but not required). Physics and technical drawing is beneficial * Previous construction experience is beneficial * Any additional tickets/training is beneficial * Full Driving Licence is required * Understanding Health and Safety compliance **What Murphy Ireland Offer In Return** Murphy Ireland pride themselves in creating opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest and most exciting construction projects. **Along With Great Opportunities/career Progression, Murphy Ireland Also Offer** * MEBSCA Rates * 21 days annual leave per annum * Large pipeline of work * Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development. * Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts, cycle to work scheme etc **Schedule** * Monday to Friday, weekend work if required * Work Location: In person both in Newbridge or on site as required by line manager **About Murphy** Operating in Ireland, the United Kingdom, Canada and the USA, Murphy provides better engineered solutions to infrastructure sectors including transportation, water, power and natural resources. So that our teams out on projects can run smoothly it is vital that we have robust support functions in place. We are committed to fostering an engaging and collaborative work environment in which each person’s career is nurtured and developed. We focus on employee development, providing a culture of innovation and positive work\-life balance. Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call Susanne Mueller on 0873423654 or email emergingtalentireland@murphygroup.ie. Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in Ireland.

title: Platform Engineer
description: You'll help build and operate the AWS platform behind assessment products used by more than 40 million learners globally. If seeing a successful Terraform plan gives you more satisfaction than it probably should, you'll fit in well here. This Dublin\-based Platform Engineer role combines infrastructure engineering, automation and ownership of large\-scale production systems, with a hybrid setup requiring three days per week in the office. **About the organisation** This organisation develops digital assessment technology used by hundreds of educational organisations worldwide. Their products help learners demonstrate knowledge and skills at scale, and they're investing heavily in the next generation of AI\-powered learning and assessment solutions. You'll join an established global business with the freedom to build new capabilities and influence how modern technologies are applied. What you'll do * Build, maintain and improve highly available, secure AWS infrastructure supporting large\-scale production systems. * Develop automation, deployment pipelines and infrastructure tooling using Terraform, Ansible, Python and GitHub Actions. * Support and enhance live platforms, identifying opportunities to improve reliability, performance and scalability. * Work closely with engineering teams to deliver infrastructure that enables frequent, dependable releases. What you need * 6\+ years' experience working with large\-scale production infrastructure in a Platform, DevOps or SRE environment. * Strong AWS expertise alongside Terraform, Docker, Linux and infrastructure automation tooling. * Solid Python skills and experience developing software beyond scripting alone. * Experience leading technical projects from planning through deployment, with strong operational discipline and documentation habits. **The practical bits** Location: Dublin City Centre Working pattern: Hybrid, minimum three days per week in the office Benefits: 25 days annual leave, pension contributions, health and dental insurance, paid sick leave, parental leave, learning and development support, commuter benefits and an employee assistance programme. Work permit sponsorship is not available

title: Platform Engineer
description: Linux Platform Engineer **Location:** South Dublin (Ideally onsite, remote considered for the right candidate) **Salary:** €75,000 – €95,000 **Experience:** 5\+ years Linux Systems Administration / Platform Engineering Linux\-first. Platform\-first. Operations\-first. We are looking for a hands\-on Linux Platform Engineer to own, operate and improve our Linux\-based platforms and services. This role is focused on Linux systems engineering, platform reliability, automation and production operations. It is not an application development role. You will work closely with engineering, infrastructure and operations teams to ensure Linux systems are secure, stable, highly available and well\-automated. We're looking for someone who is strongest at the Linux layer, comfortable living in the terminal and experienced supporting real production environments. What We're Looking For * Linux Platform Engineering * Automation \& Tooling * Container \& Platform Operations * Reliability \& Operations Essential * Linux Systems Administrator, Linux Engineer, Platform Engineer or similar role * Strong hands\-on Linux administration experience in production environments * Deep understanding of Linux internals including: * Services and processes * Networking * Storage and filesystems * User and permission management * Logging and troubleshooting * Strong Ansible experience * Strong Bash scripting skills * Experience supporting live production environments * Experience with Docker and container operations * Comfortable diagnosing issues directly on Linux systems

title: Platform Engineer (Automation) - Unannounced Project
description: Scopely is looking for a Senior Platform Engineer(Automation) to join a new truly unique multiplayer strategy game in Spain, Ireland, Portugal or the UK on a remote or hybrid basis. We can support with visa sponsorship and relocation assistance from any location. At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day \- whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily. We are in the early stages of development on an ambitious, unannounced Strategy/MMO title, creating a team of talented and passionate game makers to join us on this exciting journey! **What You’ll Do** * We are looking for a software engineer with a strong automation mindset to help improve how our engineering teams builds, tests, and deliver softwares. * This role sits at the intersection of automation, reliability, quality, and developer experience. You’ll work on the systems and tooling that enable teams to ship changes safely and repeatedly, reducing manual effort and operational risk through software. * Design, and evolve automation as internal product, to support the software delivery lifecycle, from code commit through to production * Improve the reliability, consistency, and usability of CI/CD pipelines and supporting tooling * Identify manual, error\-prone, or slow processes workflows and replace them with automation and a product\-driven approach * Collaborate with application engineers, and infrastructure teams to improve delivery safety, resilience, and operational excellence * Contribute to standards and reusable patterns for build, test, deployment, and rollback * Embed reliability, security, and quality checks into automated workflows where they add real value * Improve developer experience through better tooling, documentation, and self\-service capabilities * Use metrics and feedback to guide improvements to delivery performance and system reliability **What We’re Looking For** * Background as a software engineer, SRE, DevOps engineer, or platform engineer with a strong focus on automation * Ability to quickly navigate and understand unfamiliar codebases, proactively identifying and implementing improvements that enhance software engineering patterns (SOLID, DRY), reliability, observability, and overall system health with minimal supervision * Experience improving developer productivity or internal tooling * Experience operating systems across multiple environments (dev, test, prod) * Strong collaboration and communication skills when working across teams * Experience building or maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools such as GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or similar * Comfortable writing automation and tooling in a general\-purpose language (e.g. Python, Go, or equivalent) * Working knowledge of cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure), even if not a deep specialist * Familiarity with containerized workloads and modern deployment practices (ECS, Kubernetes/EKS, or similar) **Bonus Points** * Exposure to Site Reliability Engineering practices or operational ownership * Experience working with infrastructure as code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar) is a plus, not a requirement * Familiarity with delivery performance metrics (e.g. deployment frequency, lead time, failure rates) * Interest in software supply chain security or compliance automation **About Scopely** Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all\-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live\-operates one of the most diversified and award\-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play. Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world\-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet\-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the \#1 mobile games company in the U.S. and \#2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come. Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play. Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/. Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com. Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice. Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

title: Policy Specialist
description: Spencer Recruitment is partnering with our client, a specialist public affairs and research consultancy, to appoint a Policy Specialist/Account Manager. This is a focused, purposeful role for a driven professional ready to transition from broad project management into deep subject\-matter expertise. The successful candidate will immerse themselves in the technology and healthcare sectors, mastering the policies, frameworks, innovations, and parliamentary movements that shape them at an Irish and EU level. **About the Job** * Specialised research role embedded within a dynamic public affairs consultancy * Hybrid working model * Opportunity to develop into a recognised Subject Matter Expert in tech and health policy at an Irish and EU level **The Role** * Drive rigorous, proactive policy intelligence across the technology and healthcare sectors * Utilise, train, and refine advanced AI monitoring tools to track parliamentary debates, committee activity, and legislative drafts * Transform complex policy documents and datasets into sharp, actionable intelligence and strategic narratives * Take full ownership of research output \- from initial data extraction through to final, polished insights **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct ongoing research into regulatory shifts, legislative changes, and emerging public affairs trends in tech and health * Monitor and analyse parliamentary debates, committee work, and legislative developments at Irish and EU level * Produce clear, high\-quality written reports and briefings for internal and client use * Manage the end\-to\-end research process with accuracy and strong attention to detail * Maintain and refine AI\-powered monitoring workflows to improve intelligence gathering * Contribute to the development of strategic narratives and client\-ready policy outputs * Stay ahead of sector developments and proactively flag relevant emerging issues **About You** * 2\-4 years' experience in policy, public affairs, research, PR, or a related consultancy or agency environment * Genuine interest in how public policy, parliamentary action, and technological innovation intersect * Highly organised with a proven ability to manage projects and deliver to deadlines * Comfortable working with new software and AI\-driven monitoring tools * Entrepreneurial mindset \- proactive, comfortable with ambiguity, and driven to find answers * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to present complex legislative findings clearly and confidently * Analytically minded with a natural aptitude for research and synthesis * Thrives in a startup or fast\-moving environment **What's on Offer** * Highly competitive base salary relative to experience, designed to attract top\-tier talent * Annual performance bonus tied to individual impact and company success * Clear Subject Matter Expert career trajectory in tech and health public affairs * Travel opportunities to attend key industry conferences, policy briefings, and stakeholder events * Supportive, entrepreneurial team environment with real ownership and autonomy

title: Process Systems Engineer
description: Objective (Across UAT and Go\-Live) * Proficiently utilise multiple systems applications to drive the operational performance of the site. * Order release management into the warehouse pick environment * Real time troubleshooting * Monitoring the Execution of the Wave Management process * Monitoring of replenishments * Adhere/ Chasing of pick sequencing/ routes * Responsible for the outbound process to ensure customer orders are dispatched * Reporting and closing all defects in the UAT steps MAIN DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES Complete all UAT scenarios as assigned by line manager * Escalation of issues through documented escalation procedure * Identifying areas of improvement in day\-to\-day Operations QUALIFICATION, EXPERIENCE \& SKILLS REQUIRED * Minimum Honors Degree in an Engineering discipline * 3\- 4 years prior Process engineering experience * Experience in the Pharma/FMCG sectors an advantage * Lean six sigma qualification an advantage **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) — Projects, Infrastructure & Energy
description: **About Our Client** Our client is a leading, progressive law firm looking to welcome a Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) to their multi\-disciplinary Projects, Infrastructure \& Energy group. This is a unique, dynamic role that perfectly blends knowledge leadership with active legal practice. You will enjoy the variety of a strategic knowledge role while staying close to live client matters by carrying a manageable, reduced fee\-earning caseload. **The Role Overview** As a critical support hub for a busy and growing practice, you will provide technical know\-how, training, and support across a broad spectrum of areas, including: * Construction \& engineering * Public procurement * Energy \& natural resources * Planning \& environment * Housing \& ESG (including occasional support for the New Homes/Residential Development team) If you are a qualified solicitor who possesses a passion for technical accuracy, communicates complex legal points with clarity, and thrives on helping colleagues succeed, this role offers an exceptional platform for your career. **Key Responsibilities** * **Knowledge Management:** Develop, curate, and continuously improve the group's knowledge systems, document banks, precedents, and template advice across all core practice areas. * **Legal Analysis:** Monitor and analyze Irish and EU legislative, regulatory, and case\-law developments, translating them into practical guidance for fee earners. * **Training \& Professional Support:** Design and deliver internal training and CPD briefings for legal staff at all levels, while mentoring junior fee earners. * **Fee\-Earning Work:** Maintain a defined, manageable caseload of live matters alongside the wider team, billing in line with a reduced fee target that respects your dual responsibilities. * **Cross\-Departmental Collaboration:** Act as the first point of contact for technical queries, and collaborate with knowledge colleagues across the firm to share resources and best practices. * **Business Development:** Contribute to client\-facing articles, briefings, seminars, and thought\-leadership materials to help elevate the group's profile. **Person Specification** **Essential Criteria:** * Qualified Solicitor (Republic of Ireland) with relevant post\-qualification experience in one or more of the group's practice areas. * Strong technical research and analytical skills, with the ability to distil complex legal material into practical advice. * Exceptional drafting skills and an uncompromising eye for detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the confidence to deliver training. * Highly organized, self\-directed, and collaborative. **Desirable Criteria:** * Previous experience in a Professional Support Lawyer (PSL) or Knowledge role. * Familiarity with standard\-form construction contracts, public procurement title: Project Controls

title: Recruiting Coordinator - 6 Month Contract
description: ****🚀 Join the Future of Commerce with Whatnot!**** Whatnot is the largest livestream shopping platform in North America and Europe to buy, sell, and discover the things you love. Whether it's trading cards, fashion, electronics, or live plants, our sellers are building real businesses across hundreds of categories. We're building live commerce at a scale that's never been done in the West, and there's no playbook to copy. The people here are shaping how an entirely new industry develops. As a remote co\-located team, we're inspired by our values and anchored in hubs across the US, UK, Ireland, Poland, Germany, and Australia. We move fast, stay close to our users, and focus on the work that drives the most impact. We're one of the fastest growing marketplaces and were recently named the \#1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes. Check out the latest Whatnot updates on our news and engineering blogs and join us as we enable anyone to turn their passion into a business and bring people together through commerce. ****💻 Role**** * Own scheduling logistics across all teams and international time zones — ensuring interviews are confirmed accurately and both candidates and interviewers have all necessary details. * Collaborate closely with recruiters and hiring managers to manage complex scheduling, proactively flag conflicts, and communicate updates clearly and promptly. * Leverage Ashby for scheduling, tracking, and analytics — automating workflows where possible to improve efficiency and maintain a consistent candidate experience. * Track and report recruiting metrics to keep the hiring team informed and identify opportunities to optimize the recruiting process. * Drive recruiting enablement, including maintaining documentation, helping with trainings, and fielding ad hoc requests. * Build strong partnerships with recruiters and hiring managers to anticipate scheduling or process challenges and resolve them before they become blockers. * Contribute to team projects and initiatives, taking ownership and driving them to completion with minimal supervision. **Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance of our Dublin, Ireland hub and are open to in\-office work.** ****👋 You**** People who do well at Whatnot tend to be comfortable figuring things out as they go, biased toward action, and genuinely curious about what they're building. They care more about outcomes than credit and stay close to the product and the people using it. * Experienced: You have at least 1 year of experience as a Recruiting Coordinator, ideally in a fast\-paced or startup environment. * Highly organized: You balance multiple priorities with precision and always follow through on commitments. * Proactive and dependable: You anticipate needs, communicate effectively, and take initiative rather than waiting for direction. * Candidate\-obsessed: You care deeply about creating a smooth, human experience for every candidate and hiring partner. * Tech\-savvy: You’re comfortable using recruiting tools like Ashby — and you’re curious and resourceful when learning new systems. * Collaborative: You build trust across teams and enjoy being a helpful, reliable partner. * Growth\-minded: You welcome feedback, look for ways to improve, and lean into action to make an impact. ****💛 EOE**** Whatnot is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, parental status, disability status, or any other status protected by local law. We believe that our work is better and our company culture is improved when we encourage, support, and respect the different skills and experiences represented within our workforce. Compensation Range: €59K \- €65K

title: Recruitment Specialist (Accepting All Backgrounds)
description: * Join a booming industry in Dublin and **become a Recruitment Specialist in 2 years.** * **No recruitment experience needed** – you will learn everything from our top performers and directors. * **Join an ambitious, international** team where every day is about growth, impact, and celebrating success. **Who Are We?** Access Healthcare is one of Ireland’s fastest\-growing recruitment consultancies, delivering top\-tier talent across the healthcare industry. But growth alone isn’t what defines us. We’re building something different. A business where performance drives progression. Where ambition is matched with real opportunity. title: Regulatory Affairs Associate Level I
description: Purpose of the Position/ Role Summary Reporting to the Regulatory Affairs Team Leader, the Regulatory Affairs Associate Level I is responsible for the day to day Regulatory Affairs activities within the Regulatory Affairs Department. Key Role Specific Requirements * Prepare, compile, and submit license renewal applications in accordance with strict deadlines, and manage application through to completion. * Prepare, compile, and submit variation applications to support changes to authorised details and manage applications through to completion for national, mutual recognition, decentralised or centralised procedures. * Assist to update, prepare, compile, and submit new product applications through national, mutual recognition, decentralised or centralised procedures. * Collect, collate, and evaluate scientific data, and present the information in an organised format. * Preparation of responses to queries from regulatory agencies on new and existing products in conjunction with relevant personnel. * Liaise with Regulatory Authorities and external customers/distributors. * Work closely with all departments, providing regulatory support for the operations and control of the facility. * Inform relevant departments of changes in registered details and update internal data bases with changes and the status of on\-going tasks. * Keep up to date on information on regulatory websites as regards new guidelines, fees and application documents and inform all relevant stakeholders, where relevant. *Please note that this job description is not exhaustive, and the role holder may be required to undertake other relevant duties commensurate with the grading of the post. title: Sales Colleague
description: **Role overview:** **Sales Colleague** Navan Currys, Navan Fixed Term Contract Full Time 30\-39 hours per week, with flexibility to work additional hours. €14\.65 per hour \+ bonus (\*rising to €14\.95 on successful completion of probation period and 6 months service) At Currys we’re united by one passion: to help everyone enjoy amazing technology. This is for people who want to learn how businesses really work, how companies grow, how **Pathway to Sales, Operations, Private Equity \& Portfolio Company Leadership** DG Ventures is building a group of companies across tourism, outsourcing, media, property, transport, AI, and private equity. Sales drives everything, how operations scale, and how acquisitions create value. This is a **Business Graduate Leadership Programme** and not just a Graduate Job. This is for people who want to learn how businesses really work, how companies grow, how sales drives everything, how operations scale, and how acquisitions create value. processes. * Monitor industry trends, customer use cases, and competitive solutions within asset tracking and maintenance technologies. * Capture customer feedback and technical insights to support technical field, or equivalent professional experience. * 3\+ years of experience in a Sales Engineer, Pre\-Sales Engineer, or Solutions Engineer role, preferably within asset monitoring, industrial IoT, automation, or related technologies. * Strong technical aptitude with the ability to understand complex systems and clearly explain technical concepts to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Experience supporting enterprise B2B sales cycles, including technical discovery, solution design, and customer\-facing presentations. * Ability to articulate business value and return on investment when presenting technical solutions. * Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder\-management skills in a professional, enterprise environment. * Willingness to travel as
